objective: Create a reusable helper for shared dependency symlink setup in clean worktrees so the approved pattern from issue #70 is safe, fast, and repeatable.
scope_boundary: Add a governance-owned helper script for linking and cleaning approved dependency artifacts between a root checkout and an isolated worktree.
Enforce same-repo validation, lockfile parity checks, explicit artifact allowlisting, and safe cleanup behavior.
Document the helper as the preferred path in governance docs and close the backlog item once a real proof run succeeds.
out_of_scope: Supporting non-dependency artifacts like build outputs or environment files.
Changing package manager layouts inside product repos.
Adding product-repo-local wrapper scripts in this slice.
research_summary: Issue #70 approved the manual symlink pattern but explicitly left helper standardization open. The current governance repo already uses small bash/python helpers for repeatable operator tasks, so the right implementation is a narrow bash helper that manages only approved dependency artifacts, stores manifest state in the repo git-path instead of the worktree, and proves itself in a temporary AIS worktree.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
tasks: Add a governance helper script with link and clean commands.
Enforce same-repo validation, lockfile parity checks, and explicit artifact allowlisting.
Store cleanup manifest state outside the worktree using git-path metadata.
acceptance_criteria: There is one documented helper path for setting up shared dependency symlinks in clean worktrees.
The helper verifies same-repo and lockfile parity before linking.
Cleanup behavior is safe and only removes symlinks created through the helper.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Document, proof, and close the governance issue.
tasks: Update governance standards to reference the helper as the preferred path.
Run a real proof in a temporary ai-integration-services worktree.
Record the result in the backlog mirror and PDCA/R closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Governance docs reference the helper as the preferred path for the approved pattern.
A real proof run links, verifies, and cleans successfully in a temporary worktree.
The backlog mirror records issue #72 as done once the proof succeeds.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm that the helper should stay narrowly scoped to approved dependency artifacts and should not generalize into arbitrary symlink automation.
specialist_reviews.summary: The helper should only manage approved dependency artifacts and must preserve the policy boundaries from issue #70. Same-repo and lockfile parity checks are mandatory.

alternate_model_review.summary: This is a narrow governance helper implementation and did not require an alternate-model review to proceed.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Build the helper, proof it on a real isolated worktree, and close the governance backlog item once the behavior is verified.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Add the helper and run the real AIS worktree proof before updating the backlog mirror and closing issue #72.

material_deviation_rules: If real proof requires additional safety checks, add them in the same slice before closure.
If the helper cannot safely support an allowed artifact, narrow the allowlist or create a follow-up issue before closing.
